summary: The primary component maximizing presence in HDL among those listed is cholesterol due to its significant role over smaller amounts of triglycerides and fatty acids typically found within this type of lipoprotein particle. \boxed{\text{Cholesterol}} Agent1 reasoned that since HDL primarily transports cholesterol more so than triglycerides or fatty acids, it follows that cholesterol constitutes a larger proportion in HDL when comparing these components directly. Thus, the answer focuses on identifying which macromolecule predominates most significantly based on typical lipid profile characteristics specific to high-density lipoproteins (\(\boxed{\text{Cholesterol}}\)).
